methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $73M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same-stage peers in Biotech & Health. Range: $600M - $3.3B.

methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 5.8x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
LabGenius is a Series B biotech company founded by James Field that has raised $73M to date. Valuation analysis draws from a comparable universe of 5 private peers in the same sector and stage—Eikon Therapeutics ($3.3B), Neko Health ($1.8B), New Limit ($810M), Asimov ($800M), and Profluent ($600M)—plus 3 public biotech comps (RXRX, TEM, ONT.L). Public-comp anchoring was not applied due to absence of revenue disclosure, typical for private companies.

Two triangulation methods proved applicable. The stage-progression method, using total raised as a proxy base, applies a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical for Series B advancement, yielding $139M. The peer-median method benchmarks against the 5-company cohort's median valuation of $810M, with a documented range of $600M to $3.3B. The peer range reflects material variance driven by company-specific technology maturity and clinical progress.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $118M (low) to $931M (high), with a median of $474M. Key uncertainties include LabGenius's position relative to peer differentiation on technical capability and clinical stage, and whether early-stage financials support the upper quartile of peer valuations. The wide peer range ($600M–$3.3B) underscores stage heterogeneity within Series B biotech; additional clarity on LabGenius's technical validation and near-term milestones would narrow this band materially.
